content: A sandboxed Mac App Store app needs com.apple.security.network.server to listen on a loopback 127.0.0.1 socket, AND com.apple.security.network.client to make outgoing connections (mandatory for any outbound, including artwork fetch). Both are commonly required together. Critically, a Safari Web Extension can only talk to its own container app via native messaging (XPC through SafariWebExtensionHandler), not an arbitrary loopback WebSocket the way Chrome/Firefox content scripts can - so the loopback-WS bridge is not the idiomatic/reliable Safari path.
